---
title: "Terence Tao's ChatGPT Research Buddy"
description: "Terence Tao showed how he used ChatGPT like a research buddy — back-and-forth on a MathOverflow counterexample, hours saved, even a few math slips caught."
author: "synesis"
date: "2025-10-02"
categories: [AI, ChatGPT, research, mathematics, links]
image: cover.jpg
---

![Terence Tao. Photo: Wikimedia Commons. ([Source](https://mathstodon.xyz/@tao/115306424727150237))](cover.jpg){width=320 fig-align="center"}

Terence Tao showed how he used ChatGPT like a research buddy: instead of brute-forcing code, he had a back-and-forth to nail down parameters for a counterexample on MathOverflow. It saved him hours and even caught a few math slips along the way. You should look at the chat he had w/ ChatGPT — pretty cool!

<https://mathstodon.xyz/@tao/115306424727150237>

*Originally posted on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_terence-tao-taomathstodonxyz-activity-7379665775223164928-EKhK).*
